title: always_comb does not warn about latch
body:
Version : Verilator 4.220 2022-03-12 rev UNKNOWN.REV

Used on MACOS installed by brew

```
module test ();
    logic bit_0;
    logic bit_1;
    logic bit_2;
    logic bit_3;
    logic [7:0] vector_0;

    always_comb begin
        if (bit_0) begin
            bit_1 = 1;
        end
    end
    
    always_comb begin
        unique case (vector_0)
            8'b00000001: begin
                if (bit_0) begin
                    bit_2 = 1;
                end
            end
            default: begin
                bit_2 = 0;
            end
        endcase
    end

    always_comb begin
        unique case (vector_0)
            8'b00000001: begin
                if (bit_0) begin
                    bit_3 = 1;
                end else begin
                    bit_3 = 0;
                end
            end
            default: begin
                bit_3 = 0;
            end
        endcase
    end
endmodule
```

The only warning I get is (except for signal not driven or used)

`Latch inferred for signal 'test.bit_1' (not all control paths of combinational always assign a value)`

However bit_2 in the first always_comb block is also a latch, I suppose.
